name: Kenneth Gainwell
headline: Pass-catching insurance behind Bucky Irving's shaky health
body:
Gainwell's 2025 in Pittsburgh was the proof of concept: 148.3 fantasy points and an RB23 finish built almost entirely on receiving usage. He logged 85 targets, hauled in 73 of them for 486 yards and three scores, ran 344 routes at a 1.41 yards-per-route clip, and commanded a 16.3% target share, all career bests. As a runner he was efficient too, 537 yards at 4.71 per carry with five touchdowns, 14 broken tackles and a 55-yard long. That dual-threat profile is real, and it travels.

The catch is opportunity. In Tampa he slots behind Bucky Irving on a two-year, $14M deal, with Sean Tucker also in the mix, so standalone value is thin while Irving is healthy. But Irving is recovering from shoulder surgery and carries an injury history, which gives Gainwell a clear contingency path to RB2 numbers. Draft him as a PPR-friendly upside dart in the later rounds, not a reach.
